Ps Notice Of Motion Re Def Memo Of Costs
Off Calendar Sep-21-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF MAYRA PEREZ, MAYRA PEREZ's Motion Re Def Memo Of Costs:

Plaintiff's challenge to Defendant's memorandum of costs is denied as untimely and procedurally defective in several respects. On August 21, 2020, after entry of judgment in its favor, defendant City and County of San Francisco filed a memorandum of costs in the amount of $2,877.50. A party contesting the costs claimed in a memorandum of costs must file and serve a notice of motion to tax or strike costs within 15 days of service of the memorandum. (Cal. R. Ct. 3.1700(b)(1).) Plaintiff did not do so; instead, on September 8, 2020, she filed a "memorandum of points and authorities in support of the opposition to Defendant City and County of San Francisco's Memorandum of Costs," without the mandatory notice of motion. (Cal. R. Ct. 3.1112(a)(1).) On September 16, 2020-21 days after service of the cost memorandum, and just three days before the hearing-Plaintiff belatedly filed a "notice of motion regarding defendant CCSF's memorandum of costs" seeking "an order denying Defendant's Memorandum of Costs." (Cf. Code Civ. Proc. sec. 1005(b) [all moving and supporting papers shall be served and filed at least 16 court days before the hearing].) That notice was untimely. Failure to file and serve a motion to tax costs within the time limit waives any objection to the claimed costs. (Douglas v. Willis (1994) 27 Cal.App.4th 287, 290.) Further, in violation of Cal. R. Ct. 3.1300(c), which requires that proof of service of the moving papers must be filed no later than five court days before the hearing, plaintiff has not filed a proof of service of her opposition memorandum. Her apparent failure to serve the memorandum may explain why the City has not responded to that memorandum. Having failed to file a timely and proper motion to strike or tax costs, Plaintiff is barred from challenging the costs claimed by Defendant. (See Cal. R. Ct. 3.1700(b)(4) [after the time has passed for a motion to strike or tax costs, the clerk must immediately enter the costs on the judgment].)

Counsel for the City is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the adopted ruling.

No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.

Defendant City and County of San Francisco's demurrer is ordered OFF CALENDAR to give counsel an opportunity to comply with CCP 430.41. That law requires counsel to "meet and confer in person or by telephone" before a demurrer is filed, and attorney Hannawalt's declaration indicates that has not occurred. (Id. at 3.) The parties are ordered to meet and confer and CCSF's time to file a responsive pleading is extended to December 20, 2018.
